reviews.review_description: Downloaded the app to try but have been charged a year's subscription with no authorisation. Not cool!!!!
Couldn’t do the free trial it tried to charge me

There was obviously a lot of work put into this… But, I find it too restrictive for VR: It looks like you could walk around each environment, but there are no controls or gestures for movement; They enable your mic so you can talk to the characters, but it doesn’t matter what you say… cause if you say anything that’s not what they provide then the app doesn’t know what to do, disappointing ☹️ So, it’s kind of pointless to enable the mic audio.

Anyway, I suppose this app, with the content I viewed, would be most useful for an HR department for training HR personnel and/or employees. I think the team should put this in the store as a demo vs. asking you to subscribe before you see what it really is… cause now I have to go find how to cancel my subscription ;/
The game charges you money monthly after a woman speaks for ages only to ask for money. Overall awful game so DO NOT GET

reviews.review_title: No skip option for boring forever intros
reviews.review_description:
Who is so arrogant that they don't give an option to skip their long winded kindergarten level intros? 

Answer: The people who wrote this garbage.

I couldn't even get past the first introduction. After what seemed like 75 hours of listening to inane 'explanations' that only a 1st grader would need with no end in sight I quit the game. Uninstalled. Lamented my wasted life. Looked up if I could sue the developers for wasting my life or charge them with some type of theft. Settled on this review after that went nowhere.

I don't remember signing up for this subscription and I can't get my $30 back?

You guys suck.

This app is pretty lacking.

I'll admit I've seen 3 of your worthless lessons and spent a ttl of 20 minutes checking out the app.
But how did I sign up to give $30 for a free trial to see if you're worth a grain?

If you were worth your salt, would you have to force ppl to cancel the free trial in order to avoid automatic sign up for a yr subscription?
Prob nots.
You would ask them if they would like to continue, but we both know nobody would, because there is nothing noteworthy to gain.

I feel like a loser now.
I could have spent that and learned to sail on Marineverse or assassinated mf's on nexus.

developer_response.body: Hi BlkAsianGinger, we're genuinely sorry for the frustration you've experienced with our subscription process. Your feedback is crucial, and we're committed to improving our user experience. Please know that we are currently reviewing the policy for reimbursements and are committed to finding a solution. Thank you for bringing this to our attention, and we will follow up with an update or direct conversation to make things right for you.

reviews.review_title: Instructive and engaging way of learning
reviews.review_description: This app is a must to try. It’s good to find content like this to put on practice my soft skills in a different format than I normally do at work. The app looks beautifully designed and it’s easy to fully immerse in the experience.

Beautiful immersive learning products with next level engagement, and effectiveness. 
Instead of passively absorbing information by traditional means, I am an active participant in the experience. This immersive in experience approach is game changing! I am excited to see the upcoming content in the lobby!
An amazing, high-quality experience with relevant topics that help me progress. Looking forward to future modules!

I think this can be a great help for some people. Looking forward to really trying it out but I just hate when they slide in payment. Just say it's what it is at the beginning. $30 a year. And I definitely will pay when I'm in the market to buy a new game. But I'm not gonna be blindsided with a 1 year subscription. At least ya get the 14 day trial But I wasn't clear on how to cancel it if you did not want it once i'm sure on that I definitely will activate

developer_response.body: Hi Ayeandus - Thank you so much for your review! Please know we do take your feedback seriously and the team is considering better ways to establish clearer communication with users. Our intent has been to enable users to get a hands-on experience within the Talespin world with our Introduction experience, before deciding to start a 14 day free trial. We understand that could have and should have been more clearly stated. As we work towards incorporating your input, please note that cancelling a 14 day free trial can be done by going into Your Account in the Store left sidebar and selecting Subscriptions on your headset.

reviews.review_title: No way to refund if missed deadline.
reviews.review_description: The app itself is actually quite good (hence the 2nd star) but be wary, if you miss the cut off point for the trial you cannot get your money back! I was literally cancelling the trial this morning (I set a reminder on my phone) it charged me for the annual subscription. Since it is an in app purchase Meta cannot refund this and having to contact them directly, they are very unwilling to give a refund. Very clever to get money out of people and I know you will say should have cancelled it sooner etc and I agree and wish I did. Now £22.99 out of pocket for an app I won’t use ever again. You have been warned.

developer_response.body: Hi Lilly_Parr - We are so sorry to hear about your experience. Please know that we are currently reviewing the policy for reimbursements and are committed to finding a solution. Thank you for bringing this to our attention, and we will follow up with an update or direct conversation to make things right for you.

reviews.review_description: Waste of space, uninstalled again! No content available.

developer_response.body: Hi DaOptika, sorry to hear your experience in the app did not meet your expectations. We hope you reconsider giving Talespin another chance as we continue to build out our experiences. We are committed to delivering the best app possible!

reviews.review_description: 4.5 / 5 but rounding up b/c I haven't seen anything like this before: learning and practicing professional skills in VR. As someone who nerds out on ongoing learning for career / personal development, this is substantially more fun than an online course. Graphics are good, the situations feel pretty real (even challenging, and I think I have a decent EQ). Looking forward to taking more lessons. I did get a little lost navigating the app a couple times but nothing terrible, always found my way back to where I wanted to be. So overall: unique, worth it for me / my interests, and looking forward to seeing what other lessons are "coming soon."

The experience was really interesting and opens the mind up to what is possible, the way learning should happen. Seems much better than 2D formats. I'm keen to experience more. Is this available in schools?
I haven't seen something else quite like this. Getting introduced into a business world and navigating (and learning from) business and professional decisions kept me interested!
Would love to have a bit more to try out before buying.

reviews.review_title: Intros are too slow with no skipping
reviews.review_description: They better fix this before I leave to go walk alone with Billie Joe asking for Novocain in idiot America! I better get a response before I kill the DJ! 🙄🙄🤦

Love the app! 
As far as immersive learning content goes, this checks all the boxes. Incredible to see an app that allows you to upskill yourself or your employees effortlessly in a safe, virtual environment.

This app would be the cat's pajamas IF ONLY it would allow users to set up their own scenarios, environments, and characters! And be able to share their creations with other users in a database. It would be incredibly original. You could easily charge $9.99 per month.

reviews.review_title: Engaging Way to Practice Soft Skills
reviews.review_description: This app is a fantastic tool for honing soft skills. Its vibrant environment and engaging characters make learning enjoyable.  Can't wait to see additional scenarios.
